regions.comment: OSWORD &07 (SOUND) param block for sfx_fire: ch=&0013 amp=2 pitch=&4B dur=1
OSWORD &07 param block for sfx_hit_lo: ch=&0012 amp=2 pitch=&28 dur=1
OSWORD &07 param block for sfx_explode (sound A — noise on ch 0): ch=&0010 amp=&00F1 pitch=7 dur=&28
OSWORD &07 param block for sfx_explode (sound B — tone on ch 1): ch=&0011 amp=3 pitch=&FF dur=1
OSWORD &07 param block for sfx_score_tick: ch=&0012 amp=4 pitch=&64 dur=3
OSWORD &07 param block for sfx_level_start: ch=&0011 amp=4 pitch=&FF dur=&0F
Starfield engine state — three parallel 61-byte arrays. Loop bound is X<60 (60 active stars; the 61st slot in each array is unused). Mutated in place every frame by starfield_update in CODE.

Score: 6-byte BCD buffer, most-significant digit first. Bytes 0..3 are the four upper display digits (ten-thousands → tens of thousands → thousands → hundreds), byte 4 is the tens digit (which is what add_score_x1/x10 actually increment), byte 5 is always 0 (the trailing '0' that appears on screen — Nevryon awards score in multiples of 10). draw_score loops X=0..5 reading score,X. add_score_x1 / add_score_x10 in CODE add zp_score_acc (or zp_score_acc×10) at byte 4 / byte 3 with carry propagating upward through bytes 3..0.

High score: 6-byte BCD buffer, same layout as `score`. Initialised to all zero on disk. Compared against `score` by check_new_high_score after each life lost and after each stage clear; if the current score is higher, copied over byte-by-byte. Rendered by draw_high_score onto the stage-clear screen.

comments.0x2800: Play 'fire' sound via OSWORD &07. Loads the param block at &2809 (ch=&0013 amp=2 pitch=&4B dur=1).
comments.0x2811: Play 'hit/low' sound. Param block at &281A (ch=&0012 amp=2 pitch=&28 dur=1).
comments.0x2822: Play 'explode' sound — two OSWORD &07 calls in sequence. Sound A (noise on ch 0) via params at &283B; sound B (tone on ch 1) via params at &2843. Note: amp.lo of sound A is at &283D = the byte that Loader3 PATCHES with `?&283D = 256 - V%` (the user's volume preference from options.BAS).
comments.0x28D5: Two-byte scratch slot (&28D5..&28D6). Currently unused by the engine — likely paired storage left over for whatever scratch_28D3 / scratch_28D4 hold.
comments.0x28D7:
Per-subframe (from game_step's play_subframe): if force_pod_state == 1, animate the floating force-pod power-up — a chomping ball drawn at (force_pod_x, force_pod_y). Erases the previous frame with lev_erase_brush + &80 (2 byte-col × &0C lines), then plots one of 6 frames at gfx_ball_frame0..5 (each 2×&0C = &18 bytes).

Frame selection: inline multiply-by-&18 — `LDA #LO(gfx_ball_frame0 - &18); CLC; LDX force_pod_frame; loop ADC #&18 / DEX / BNE` — so frame N=1 gives &60 (= gfx_ball_frame0), N=2 gives &78, ..., N=6 gives &D8. HI is hard-wired to &38.

After plot, force_pod_frame INCs (wraps 7→1). Also checks (a) OSBYTE &81 / -1 INKEY for any key press, and (b) a box-collision against the player ship — either condition dismisses the pod (jumps to L299F, presumably to collect or hide).
comments.0x28D8: 200-frame counter used only by play_death_sequence. The byte is the LO operand of force_pod_anim's first instruction (`LDA force_pod_state` at &28D7..&28D9) — i.e. this scratch is aliased onto live code. Safe because play_death_sequence terminates the game; CODE2 is re-loaded from disk on the next stage-start, restoring the original operand byte. INCed each frame; loop exits at &C8 (= 200 frames at 50 Hz).
comments.0x29D8: 15 bytes of NOP padding (&EA) between update_hazard_missile and draw_score. Not referenced by anything.
comments.0x2EFE: 1-byte toggle used by the muzzle-flash plot during the ship-intro animation. Reset to 0 at the start of intro_or_scoreboard (&2F0B), then EOR'd with 1 each time the muzzle-flash sprite is drawn. While the toggle is 1, the plot uses gfx_muzzle_flash_frame1 (sprite_src_lo overridden to &C0); while 0, it uses the gfx_muzzle_flash_frame0 + &10 partial sprite. Initial on-disk value is &EA (NOP) — only the &2F0B init makes the toggle path actually fire. The byte sits in the gap between the sfx_level_start OSWORD parameter block (&2EF6..&2EFD) and the next routine entry at &2EFF.
comments.0x29E7: Draw the 6-digit score at char-col 9, row &5F. Sets up the screen address then tail-falls into draw_score_digits_loop with X = 0.
comments.0x29F0:
Iterate the 6-byte BCD score buffer, calling draw_score_digit per byte. Shared by draw_score and draw_high_score's render path.

Inputs:
  X = starting index (caller pre-sets to 0)
  Source buffer: hardwired to `score` here (compare draw_high_score which uses high_score). The two paths happen to share this loop body because both buffers are 6 bytes wide and laid out the same way.
  Current screen address: set up by caller (via calc_screen_addr).

Uses `scratch_28D3` to save X across the draw_score_digit call (X is overwritten by the digit-sprite selector). Loops until CPX #&06.
comments.0x2A08:
Draw one score digit at the current screen position.

Inputs: A = digit value (0..9), X = column offset.
Selects gfx_digit_N via `gfx_digit_0 + 8*N` and plots a 4×8 sprite.
comments.0x2A20: Try to spawn a new hazard missile in one of the two slots (hazard_missile_x[0..1] == &FF = free). Called from CODE's hazard_type_dispatch for hazard_type &06. Validates the firer's hazard_x is in [&08, &25] (on-screen), copies hazard_x + 2 / hazard_y into the missile slot, mirrors hazard_flip into hazard_missile_flip (controls vertical direction in step_hazard_missile), and sets hazard_missile_homing_dir from a player-X comparison: 1 if player_x + 3 >= missile_x (player to the right of the missile → home rightward), 0 otherwise (home leftward). Plays sfx_score_tick to mark the launch.\n\nThe missile sprite is `gfx_icon_missile_rocket` — a 4×6-px yellow-and-red rocket pointing vertically.
comments.0x2AA2: Per-frame: step each of the 2 hazard missiles (slots 0 and 1) then collision-check each against the player. Inactive slots (hazard_missile_x == &FF) are skipped.
comments.0x2AC4:
Step one hazard missile (X = slot index 0..1). Erases the old 1×6 position with lev_erase_brush + &80, then advances:
  horizontally by hazard_missile_homing_dir (1 = INC X by 1, else DEC X by 2 — homes on the player)
  vertically by hazard_missile_flip (1 = Y+=2, else Y-=2)

Bounds-checks the new (X, Y) against the playfield; out-of-bounds → mark slot inactive. Plots gfx_icon_missile_rocket (4×6-px rocket) at the new position.
comments.0x2B48: Box-collision check between one hazard_missile slot and the player. On hit, jumps to the player-death path (lose_a_life via the CODE-side handler).
comments.0x2BA4: Level-start GET READY! intro. Draws the score, plots 7 gfx_icon_lives_intro sprites at row &57 col 8..&F, shutter-fades to a black star field, plays sfx_explode for drama, then loops 75 frames updating starfield and blitting gfx_text_get_ready (split as two 8-col halves at cols 12 and 20) before erasing.
comments.0x2CCA: Draw the title screen (logo, scores, copyright) using the doubled-font printer in CODE3 + a sequence of pre-rendered text sprites from GRAPHIX.
comments.0x2E3F: Render the 6-digit high_score buffer onto the screen at (col &11, row &96). Loops X = 0..5 reading high_score[X] and calls draw_score_digit per byte (same render style as draw_score, just from a different buffer at a different screen position). Called from the stage-clear / game-over UI.
comments.0x2E60: Compare current `score` against `high_score` byte-by-byte MSD-first. If the current score is strictly greater (any score byte > the matching high_score byte before equality runs out), copies all 6 bytes of score into high_score so subsequent draw_high_score calls show the new value.\n\nUsed at game-over / stage-clear to roll the leaderboard.
comments.0x2E83: Beep that plays during the BONUS countdown roll-up (called from stage_clear_screen).
comments.0x2E94: Play the death sequence sound effects (a 3-tone descending pitch followed by a noise burst).
comments.0x2EED: Play 'level start / pickup' sound. Param block at &2EF6 (ch=&0011 amp=4 pitch=&FF dur=&0F).
comments.0x2EFF: Branch: if lives_left == 6 (fresh game, full lives) → ship_intro_anim; otherwise → draw_final_scoreboard. Called from main_init after the score restore.
comments.0x2F09: Plays the four-frame ship reveal animation (right-to-left): the player ship at lev_player_sprite is plotted progressively wider from col 5 → col 2 over four vsyncs.
comments.0x2F54: Setup helper for ship_intro_frame_*: wait a frame, then calc_screen_addr at (0, &C8), set sprite_src_hi=&4E (LEVD1), Y=&16 (22 lines).
comments.0x2F66: Run one frame: starfield_update + ~256*60 cycle delay + OSBYTE &13 (wait vsync).
comments.0x30F8: Save current score and high-score to the loader persistence area at page &0CF3+. Called when handing off to the BASIC loader between stages.\n\nEffects: writes loader_lives_save = lives_left, loader_score_save (6 bytes) = score[0..5], loader_hiscore_save (6 bytes) = the high-score buffer.
comments.0x3115: Restore score / high-score from the loader persistence area into lives_left, score[0..5], and the high-score buffer. Called by main_init at game start.
comments.0x3170: PAUSE handler. Loops on `OSBYTE &81 / KEY_PAUSE` until the key is released, then loops again until KEY_PAUSE is pressed once more to resume. Returns when un-paused.
comments.0x31B1: End-of-game scoreboard display. Calls shutter_fade then a sequence of ship/text plot helpers; followed by a 6-iteration plot loop. Triggered when lives_left != 6 in intro_or_scoreboard.
